name: GT-jb-positive-spectrum
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:2492 (3.3.3), VERBATIM: "Let A be a JB algebra. An element  $a \in \tilde{A}$  is called positive, written  $a \ge 0$ , if Sp  $a \subset [0, \infty)$ . By the spectral theorem (3.2.4)  $a \ge 0$  if and only if a is a square." Positivity is INTRINSIC (defined by the spectrum, computed in C(a)); for c in the subalgebra C(y) of V, c>=0 in V iff Sp c subset [0,infty) iff phi(c)>=0 pointwise on Sp y under the isomorphism of GT-jb-spectral. The order unit space C(Sp y) has cone = pointwise-nonnegative functions (HOS:474 norm/order on C_0(X)).
